messages.content:
You transcribe between Byzantine (Chrysanthine / New Analytical Method) notation and Western staff notation.

Convert the input notation to the requested target format. Preserve musical meaning: melodic contour, mode (echos), martyria, ison (drone), microtonal alterations (diesis, fthora), and rhythmic neume modifiers (gorgon, argon). Do NOT round to 12-TET without marking approximation. Do NOT add harmony or impose 4/4.

Byzantine → Western: state mode/Ni, staff pitches (D4, E4…), preserve ison line, mark microtones.
Western → Byzantine: martyria, interval names (oligon, petastē, apostrophos…), ison as (Ν)/(Δι)/(Κε).

Output notation only — no commentary.

Direction: Byzantine → Western
[Mode I (First Authentic), martyria: Ni = Πα (Pa)]
(Ν) ison
oligon | oligon | petastē | oligon | kentēma

Mode I (First Authentic), Ni = D4
Ison: D4
D4 E4 F#4 G4 A4

Western → Byzantine: martyria, interval names (oligon, petastē, apostrophos…), ison as (Ν)/(Δι)/(Κε).

Output notation only — no commentary.

Direction: Byzantine → Western
[Mode I, Ni = Πα]
(Ν) ison
kentēma | apostrophos | apostrophos | oligon

Mode I, Ni = D4
Ison: D4
A4 G4 F#4 E4

Output notation only — no commentary.

Direction: Byzantine → Western
[Mode II (Second Authentic), soft chromatic, Ni = Δι]
(Δι) ison
oligon (diesis) | petastē | apostrophos

Mode II soft chromatic, Ni = G4
Ison: G4
G4 G4↑ A4↓ G4
(G4↑ ≈ G + ~33 cents; A4↓ ≈ A - ~33 cents per analytical transcription)

(Κε) ison
ison | oligon | oligon | petastē
— martyria shift —
[Mode II, Ni = Κε]
(Ζω) ison
apostrophos | oligon

Mode II, Ni = A4
Ison: A4 → B4 (mid-phrase ison shift to Zo)
A4 A4 B4 C5
Ison: B4
A4 B4
